title: skopeo sync does not have tag filtering options, or `--include-tag-regex` and `--exclude-tag-regex`
body:
We need to sync a repository from docker.io to our region's Docker mirror, which is a paid service. We have some tags for testing that don't need to be synced, so a tag filtering option would be ideal.

I've carefully reviewed the documentation and found that YAML can be used for configuration, but this is too unintuitive and elegant, and not convenient for CI/CD.

I'm considering submitting a pull request for this feature. Does the author intend to include this feature, or do they have any guidelines or suggestions?

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

As a general matter, the CLI should not be _more_ powerful than YAML; it would be too confusing to have some features that are only available in one, and other features that are only available in the other. So a new feature, if any, should definitely include a YAML version first (or only a YAML version).

(As for “YAML is unintuitive and not elegant”, that can always be hidden in a dedicated shell script or another helper.)

…  then we run into the difficulty that the existing YAML format is fairly inflexible, there is no way to add _options_ to some of the existing YAML features. Adding a tag exclusion in a reasonably consistent way does seem possible, from a quick glance, as an additional filter on top of the currently-determined list of tags. (Alternatively, restructuring the YAML to allow more options and the like would be a much larger project, not something very likely to happen in the short term.)

I’m rather more worried about adding regex filtering to the CLI — a linear list of options is an even more limited interface. Also we should not have two entirely different filtering implementations: that sort of implies that the non-YAML input case would need to be mapped into the YAML case internally… a plausible refactoring, but a bit disruptive.
Okay, thank you for your reply.
